ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF. Original certificate on page 1, Copy on pages 2-3. All text clearly readable.

invoice.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF, 7 pages. Issued by FlexLink AB. All text clearly readable.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The remarks field contains 'C024.10400-1' which is a project/order reference, not an LC reference.

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Consignor 'Flexlink AB' identified in invoice footer on all pages as 'FlexLink AB, Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 Gothenburg, Sweden'.
pairing_validation.motivation: Consignor Flexlink AB is identifiable in the invoice via the footer block on every page. The certificate does not reference a specific invoice number, but consignor match is sufficient for pairing validation.

consignor.invoice_value: FlexLink AB, Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 Gothenburg, Sweden (footer on all pages)

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'Flexlink AB' in all invoice pages
comparison_steps.details: The name 'FlexLink AB' appears in the footer of every page of the invoice (pages 1-7) with full address: 'FlexLink AB, Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 Gothenburg, Sweden'. After normalization of case, 'Flexlink AB' matches 'FlexLink AB'. The address matches: Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05, and the country is Sweden in both documents. Note: The Invoice Address field shows 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' in Malaysia, which is a different legal entity, but the consignor 'FlexLink AB' is explicitly present in the invoice footer.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure - consignor name found explicitly in invoice footer
Land verification via explicit address in footer: Sweden

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Flexlink AB' in Sweden. The invoice's Invoice Address is 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' in Malaysia, which is a different legal entity. However, per rule 4.1.0, the consignor name 'FlexLink AB' with address 'Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 Gothenburg, Sweden' appears explicitly in the footer of every page of the invoice. After case normalization, the names match exactly. The country 'Sweden' is explicitly stated in the footer address. Therefore MATCH via 4.1.0.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignor is FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD in Malaysia
rejected_hypotheses.reason: This is the Invoice Address party, not the consignor. The certificate's consignor 'Flexlink AB' is a different legal entity found in the footer.

consignee.certificate_value: HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC, 2nd Floor, Tower A Mandarin Garden 2, No 99 Tan Mai Street, Tuong Mai Ward, 10000 HA NOI CITY, VIETNAM
consignee.invoice_value: Invoice Address: FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD, Malaysia. Shipping Address: MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.

comparison_steps.description: Check if consignee 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' appears in Invoice Address field
comparison_steps.details: Invoice Address is 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' in Malaysia. 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' does not appear in this field.

comparison_steps.description: Check if consignee 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' appears in Shipping Address field
comparison_steps.details: Shipping Address is 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY' at Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' does not appear in this field. The company names are entirely different: 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY' vs 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM'.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' anywhere in the invoice
comparison_steps.details: Full text search of all 7 invoice pages reveals no occurrence of 'HOP LONG' or 'TECHNOLOGY JSC' anywhere in the invoice. The name is entirely absent from the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee appears in Ship-To field
comparison_steps.details: The Ship-To field contains 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY', not 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC'. This rule cannot apply.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification - primary invoicing field checked first
Search in all invoice address/identification blocks
MISMATCH - consignee cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' in Hanoi, Vietnam. The invoice contains two named parties: (1) Invoice Address: 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' in Malaysia, (2) Shipping Address: 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY' in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Neither of these is 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC'. A full text search of all 7 invoice pages confirms that the string 'HOP LONG' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. The consignee in the certificate cannot be identified in the invoice. Per rule 4.2.4, this is MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC could be related to MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY because both are in Vietnam
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Per rule 4.1.3.1 (applied to consignee), the system may NOT assume corporate relationships, agent relationships, or trading relationships. These are entirely different company names. Being in the same country is not sufficient to establish identity.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD could be the consignee since it is the Invoice Address
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Certificate names 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' as consignee, not 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD'. These are entirely different entities.

goods_description.certificate_value: CONVEYOR PARTS - 21 article numbers including XBBP 45A180R10, XBCB 3A180, XBCB LA180, XBCR 25 H, XBEB A180 (FLXC4247801), XBEJ A180, XBMR 170, XBTP 3A180 K, XLAG 5, XLRD 36 Q, XLRE 15, XLRF 64 Q, XLRJ 100, XLRK 65 HQ, XLRL 110 VQ, XLRS 3X15, XLRT 3X33 D, XLRZ Q, XMMJ 6, XSCJ 6X160, 3920500
goods_description.invoice_value: Invoice contains different article numbers: XBCE 40, XCAC 25 P, XCBE 44X64, XCBL 3X44X64, XCBL 3X64, XCFA 44 B, XCFF 64X210, XLBH 90A65R150, XLBV 30A65R300, XLBV 45A65R1000, XLCB 3A65, XLCR 25, FLXC4258185 XLEB A65, FLXC4258186 XLEB A65, FLXC4258187 XLEB A65, FLXC4258188 XLEB A65, XLEJ A65, XLEJ A65 S, XLRE 15-33P, XLRF 64 Q, XLRK 45 HQ, XLRL 60 VQ, XLRZ Q, XLTP 5A65, FLXC4258189 XLTP 5A65 FP, 3923518, FLXC4258190 5995933

comparison_steps.description: Match each certificate article number against invoice article numbers
comparison_steps.details: Systematic comparison of certificate article numbers vs invoice article numbers: XBBP 45A180R10 → NOT in invoice. XBCB 3A180 → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLCB 3A65, different). XBCB LA180 → NOT in invoice. XBCR 25 H → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLCR 25, different prefix). XBEB A180 (FLXC4247801) → NOT in invoice (invoice has FLXC4258185-4258188 XLEB A65, different codes). XBEJ A180 → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLEJ A65/XLEJ A65 S, different prefix). XBMR 170 → NOT in invoice. XBTP 3A180 K → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLTP 5A65). XLAG 5 → NOT in invoice. XLRD 36 Q → NOT in invoice. XLRE 15 → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLRE 15-33P, different suffix). XLRF 64 Q → FOUND in invoice line 210. XLRJ 100 → NOT in invoice. XLRK 65 HQ → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLRK 45 HQ, different model). XLRL 110 VQ → NOT in invoice (invoice has XLRL 60 VQ, different model). XLRS 3X15 → NOT in invoice. XLRT 3X33 D → NOT in invoice. XLRZ Q → FOUND in invoice line 240. XMMJ 6 → NOT in invoice. XSCJ 6X160 → NOT in invoice. 3920500 → NOT in invoice (invoice has 3923518, different number). Of 21 certificate articles, only 2 (XLRF 64 Q, XLRZ Q) can be identified in the invoice. 19 articles cannot be found. The certificate and invoice clearly describe DIFFERENT sets of conveyor parts.

rules_applied.description: Priority 1 - Article number matching. Certificate contains article numbers which must be matched via exact identification.
MISMATCH - 19 out of 21 certificate article numbers cannot be identified in the invoice

goods_description.debug_explanation: The certificate lists 21 specific conveyor part article numbers. The invoice lists a completely different set of conveyor part article numbers. While both documents describe 'CONVEYOR PARTS' from FlexLink, the specific articles are almost entirely different. Only 2 of 21 article numbers (XLRF 64 Q and XLRZ Q) appear in both documents. Per rule 4.3.2, article numbers must be matched exactly. Since 19 of 21 certificate articles cannot be found in the invoice, this is a clear MISMATCH. The certificate references project C024.10400-1 while the invoice references project C024.10402, confirming these are different orders/shipments.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Both are 'CONVEYOR PARTS' so the goods description matches at a general level
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Per rule 4.3.2, when article numbers are present in the certificate, matching must occur via exact article number identification (Priority 1). The general category 'CONVEYOR PARTS' is insufficient when specific article numbers are listed and do not match.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Some article prefixes are similar (XB vs XL series)
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Article numbers must be matched exactly per 4.3.2. Similar prefixes do not constitute a match. XBCB 3A180 is not the same as XLCB 3A65.

quantity.invoice_value: 27 line items with different article numbers and quantities; the few overlapping articles have different quantities (e.g. XLRF 64 Q: cert 66 PCS vs invoice 65 PC; XLRZ Q: cert 140 PCS vs invoice 130 PC)

comparison_steps.description: Attempt to verify certificate quantities against invoice
comparison_steps.details: Since the article numbers in the certificate and invoice are almost entirely different (as established in goods description), the per-article quantities cannot be verified. For the two overlapping articles: XLRF 64 Q has 66 PCS in certificate vs 65 PC in invoice (mismatch); XLRZ Q has 140 PCS in certificate vs 130 PC in invoice (mismatch). The quantities for corresponding articles do not match.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check - certificate quantities cannot be identified in invoice due to different article sets
MISMATCH - quantities cannot be verified and where overlap exists, values differ

quantity.debug_explanation: The certificate lists per-article quantities for 21 articles, but 19 of these articles do not exist in the invoice. For the 2 articles that do overlap (XLRF 64 Q and XLRZ Q), the quantities differ: certificate says 66 PCS for XLRF 64 Q while invoice says 65 PC; certificate says 140 PCS for XLRZ Q while invoice says 130 PC. Both differences exceed the ±0.1% tolerance. This is MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Total piece count could be compared
rejected_hypotheses.reason: The articles are different between documents, making total comparisons meaningless. Summation of different products is not permitted.

country_of_origin.certificate_value: Poland, Sweden, Germany, Denmark, Estonia, China, Czech Republic
country_of_origin.invoice_value: Per-article CoO: Poland, Sweden, China, Estonia, Czech Republic, Lithuania

comparison_steps.description: Check each certificate origin country against invoice CoO fields
comparison_steps.details: Certificate lists 7 origin countries. Invoice per-article CoO fields contain: Poland (lines 10, 20, 90, 140, 150, 160, 170, 190, 250, 260, 280), Sweden (lines 30, 40, 50, 60, 100, 110, 120, 130, 200, 270), China (lines 70, 80), Estonia (lines 210, 230, 240), Czech Republic (line 180), Lithuania (line 220). Certificate countries found in invoice: Poland ✓, Sweden ✓, China ✓, Estonia ✓, Czech Republic ✓. Certificate countries NOT found in invoice: Germany ✗, Denmark ✗. Additionally, invoice contains Lithuania which is NOT listed in certificate box 3.

comparison_steps.description: Each certificate origin country must be identifiable in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Germany and Denmark are listed as origin countries in the certificate but do not appear in any CoO field in the invoice. Per rule 4.5.5, each origin country in the certificate must be expressly identifiable in the invoice. Two are missing → MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 Check for non-certificate origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Invoice contains Lithuania as CoO which is not in certificate box 3
comparison_steps.details: Invoice line 220 (XLRK 45 HQ) has CoO: Lithuania. Lithuania is NOT listed in certificate box 3. The certificate lists specific non-EU countries (China) alongside EU countries. Lithuania is an EU member, but the certificate explicitly names specific countries rather than using 'EU' as a collective term. However, since Germany and Denmark are already missing from the invoice, this additional discrepancy reinforces the MISMATCH conclusion. Note: The certificate and invoice clearly cover different shipments/orders.

rules_applied.description: Origin identification in invoice - Germany and Denmark not found
Positive verification requirement - not all certificate origins identifiable in invoice

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 lists 7 origin countries: Poland, Sweden, Germany, Denmark, Estonia, China, Czech Republic. The invoice contains per-article CoO fields listing Poland, Sweden, China, Estonia, Czech Republic, and Lithuania. Germany and Denmark from the certificate cannot be found in any invoice CoO field. Additionally, Lithuania appears in the invoice but not in the certificate. Per rule 4.5.5, each certificate origin must be expressly identifiable in the invoice - Germany and Denmark fail this test. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Germany and Denmark might be covered by EU normalization
rejected_hypotheses.reason: The certificate does not use 'EU' or 'European Union' as an origin designation in box 3. It lists specific countries. EU normalization (4.5.4.1) applies when 'EU' is used as a collective term. Here specific countries are named and must each be found in the invoice.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Document pairing - certificate file contains Original (p1) and Copy (p2-3); invoice file contains 7-page invoice

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: PASS - Consignor 'Flexlink AB' found in invoice footer

rule_evaluation_log.description: Corporate structure - consignor name search in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH - 'FlexLink AB' found in invoice footer with matching address and country Sweden
MISMATCH - HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC not found in Invoice Address (FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD) or Shipping Address (MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY) or anywhere else in invoice

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MISMATCH - 2 of 7 certificate origin countries missing from invoice

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet och fakturan avser tydligt olika leveranser. Mottagaren i certifikatet är 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' i Hanoi, Vietnam, men denna part förekommer inte alls i fakturan som istället är ställd till 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' i Malaysia med leverans till 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM' i Ho Chi Minh City. Dessutom innehåller certifikatet och fakturan helt olika artikelnummer (t.ex. certifikatet listar XBBP 45A180R10, XBCB 3A180 m.fl. medan fakturan listar XBCE 40, XCAC 25 P m.fl.), och ursprungsländerna Tyskland och Danmark som anges i certifikatet saknas i fakturan.

consistency_check.explanation: 4 checkpoints have MISMATCH status. comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL which is consistent with rule 6.1 (at least one MISMATCH → NOT_IDENTICAL).

human_readable_report.title: Verification Report - Certificate of Origin vs Invoice

sections.invoice_value: FlexLink AB, Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 Gothenburg, Sweden (footer all pages)
sections.rule_applied: Corporate structure - consignor name explicitly found in invoice footer with matching address and country
sections.motivation: Consignor 'Flexlink AB' from certificate matches 'FlexLink AB' in invoice footer (case normalization). Address Byfogdegatan 11, 415 05 and country Sweden match. Invoice Address is a different entity (FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD, Malaysia) but consignor is explicitly present via footer.

sections.certificate_value: HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC, 2nd Floor, Tower A Mandarin Garden 2, No 99 Tan Mai Street, Tuong Mai Ward, 10000 HA NOI CITY, VIETNAM
sections.invoice_value: Invoice Address: FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD, Malaysia. Shipping Address: MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.
sections.rule_applied: Consignee company name not identifiable anywhere in invoice
sections.motivation: Certificate consignee 'HOP LONG TECHNOLOGY JSC' in Hanoi, Vietnam cannot be found anywhere in the 7-page invoice. Invoice Address is 'FLEXLINK ENGINEERING SDN BHD' (Malaysia) and Shipping Address is 'MONDELEZ KINH DO VIET NAM JOINT STOCK COMPANY' (Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam). Neither matches. Full text search confirms absence.

sections.certificate_value: CONVEYOR PARTS - 21 articles: XBBP 45A180R10, XBCB 3A180, XBCB LA180, XBCR 25 H, XBEB A180, XBEJ A180, XBMR 170, XBTP 3A180 K, XLAG 5, XLRD 36 Q, XLRE 15, XLRF 64 Q, XLRJ 100, XLRK 65 HQ, XLRL 110 VQ, XLRS 3X15, XLRT 3X33 D, XLRZ Q, XMMJ 6, XSCJ 6X160, 3920500
sections.invoice_value: CONVEYOR project C024.10402 - 27 articles: XBCE 40, XCAC 25 P, XCBE 44X64, XCBL 3X44X64, XCBL 3X64, XCFA 44 B, XCFF 64X210, XLBH 90A65R150, XLBV 30A65R300, XLBV 45A65R1000, XLCB 3A65, XLCR 25, XLEB A65 (x4), XLEJ A65, XLEJ A65 S, XLRE 15-33P, XLRF 64 Q, XLRK 45 HQ, XLRL 60 VQ, XLRZ Q, XLTP 5A65, XLTP 5A65 FP, 3923518, 5995933
sections.rule_applied: Article number exact matching - 19 of 21 certificate article numbers not found in invoice
sections.motivation: Certificate contains 21 specific article numbers. Only 2 (XLRF 64 Q and XLRZ Q) can be identified in the invoice. The remaining 19 articles (XBBP 45A180R10, XBCB 3A180, etc.) are entirely absent from the invoice. The certificate references project C024.10400-1 while the invoice references C024.10402, confirming different orders.

sections.certificate_value: Per-article PCS quantities for 21 items (e.g., XBBP 45A180R10: 2 PCS, XBCB 3A180: 9 PCS, etc.)
sections.invoice_value: Different articles with different quantities. Overlapping articles: XLRF 64 Q: 65 PC (cert: 66 PCS); XLRZ Q: 130 PC (cert: 140 PCS)
sections.rule_applied: Occurrence check - quantities for different article sets cannot be verified; overlapping articles have different quantities
sections.motivation: 19 of 21 certificate articles do not exist in the invoice, so their quantities cannot be verified. For the 2 overlapping articles, quantities differ: XLRF 64 Q is 66 PCS in certificate vs 65 PC in invoice; XLRZ Q is 140 PCS in certificate vs 130 PC in invoice. Both exceed ±0.1% tolerance.

sections.certificate_value: Poland, Sweden, Germany, Denmark, Estonia, China, Czech Republic
sections.invoice_value: Per-article CoO: Poland, Sweden, China, Estonia, Czech Republic, Lithuania
sections.rule_applied: Positive verification - Germany and Denmark listed in certificate but absent from all invoice CoO fields
sections.motivation: Certificate box 3 lists 7 origin countries. Germany and Denmark cannot be found in any CoO field in the invoice. The invoice additionally lists Lithuania which is absent from the certificate. 5 of 7 certificate origins verified (Poland, Sweden, China, Estonia, Czech Republic), but 2 are missing.

human_readable_report.summary: The certificate and invoice clearly relate to DIFFERENT shipments/orders from the same company (FlexLink AB). The consignor matches (FlexLink AB, Sweden), but the consignee, article numbers, quantities, and origin countries all differ significantly. The certificate covers project C024.10400-1 while the invoice covers project C024.10402. Result: NOT_IDENTICAL.
